name: pan (goat-legged nature god)
shortDescription: Pan is the Greek god of the wild, shepherds, flocks, rustic music, and impromptus. He is often depicted with the hindquarters, legs, and horns of a goat.
description: Pan is the Greek god of the wild, shepherds, flocks, rustic music, and impromptus. He is often depicted with the hindquarters, legs, and horns of a goat, a symbol of his connection to the untamed wilderness. His birthplace is traditionally Arcadia, a mountainous region in the Peloponnese known for its pastoral beauty. Pan is a son of Hermes, although some accounts list his parents as Dryope and Hermes or Penelope and all the suitors of Odysseus. He is a jovial god, fond of music and dance, and is often associated with revelry and ecstatic experiences. He is known for his sudden appearances, which could inspire either joy or terror, hence the word 'panic'. While not a major Olympian deity, Pan was deeply revered by rural populations who depended on his protection for their flocks and livelihoods. He is often depicted playing the syrinx, or panpipes, a musical instrument he invented. The death of Pan was famously proclaimed by a sailor, marking a symbolic end to the age of paganism in some interpretations. He embodies the untamed aspects of nature, the power of music, and the unpredictable forces that govern the wild.
